ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "BusinessEntityID" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "FirstName" TEXT /* First name of the employee */,
  "LastName" TEXT /* Last name of the employee */,
  "Email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "Phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the employee */,
  "Address" TEXT /* Street address of the employee */,
  "City" TEXT /* City of the employee */,
  "State" TEXT /* State of the employee */,
  "Country" TEXT /* Country of the employee */,
  "DateOfBirth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the employee */,
  "HireDate" TEXT /* Date when the employee was hired */,
  "JobTitle" TEXT /* Job title of the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("BusinessEntityID")
)

CREATE TABLE "pay_rates" (
  "BusinessEntityID" INTEGER /* Employee identification number. Foreign key to Employee.BusinessEntityID. */,
  "RateChangeDate" TEXT /* Date the change in pay is effective. */,
  "Rate" TEXT /* Salary hourly rate. */,
  "PayFrequency" TEXT /* 1 = Salary received monthly, 2 = Salary received biweekly. */,
  "ModifiedDate" TEXT /* Date and time the record was last updated. */,
  "PayType" TEXT /* Type of pay (e.g., hourly, salary) */,
  "Currency" TEXT /* Currency of the pay rate (e.g., USD, EUR) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("BusinessEntityID", "RateChangeDate")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "DepartmentID" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department. */,
  "DepartmentName" TEXT /* Name of the department. */,
  "ManagerID" INTEGER /* Employee ID of the department manager (Foreign key to employees). */,
  "DepartmentCode" TEXT /* Short code for the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("DepartmentID"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_ManagerID FOREIGN KEY ("ManagerID") REFERENCES employees ("BusinessEntityID")
)

CREATE TABLE "positions" (
  "PositionID" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each position. */,
  "PositionName" TEXT /* Title of the job position. */,
  "DepartmentID" INTEGER /* Reference to the department to which the position belongs. */,
  "PositionCode" TEXT /* Short code for the position */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("PositionID"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_positions_DepartmentID FOREIGN KEY ("DepartmentID") REFERENCES departments ("DepartmentID")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_positions" (
  "BusinessEntityID" INTEGER /* Employee identification number (Foreign key to employees). */,
  "PositionID" INTEGER /* Position identifier (Foreign key to positions). */,
  "StartDate" TEXT /* Date when the employee started in this position. */,
  "EndDate" TEXT /* Date when the employee left this position. */,
  "JobDescription" TEXT /* Description of the job responsibilities */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("BusinessEntityID", "PositionID")
)

CREATE TABLE "pay_frequencies" (
  "PayFrequencyID" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the pay frequency. */,
  "FrequencyDescription" TEXT /* Description of the pay frequency (e.g., Monthly, Biweekly). */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("PayFrequencyID")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "AuditLogID" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry. */,
  "BusinessEntityID" INTEGER /* Employee identification number (Foreign key to employees). */,
  "ChangeDate" TEXT /* Date when the change was made. */,
  "ChangeDescription" TEXT /* Description of the change made. */,
  "ChangedBy" TEXT /* Username of the person who made the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("AuditLogID"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_BusinessEntityID FOREIGN KEY ("BusinessEntityID") REFERENCES employees ("BusinessEntityID")
)

CREATE TABLE "benefits" (
  "BenefitID" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each benefit. */,
  "BenefitName" TEXT /* Name of the benefit. */,
  "PositionID" INTEGER /* Reference to the position associated with this benefit. */,
  "BenefitType" TEXT /* Type of benefit (e.g., medical, dental, vision) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("BenefitID"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_benefits_PositionID FOREIGN KEY ("PositionID") REFERENCES positions ("PositionID")
)

CREATE TABLE "leave_types" (
  "LeaveTypeID" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the leave type. */,
  "LeaveTypeDescription" TEXT /* Description of the leave type (e.g., Vacation, Sick Leave). */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("LeaveTypeID")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_leaves" (
  "EmployeeLeaveID" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each leave entry. */,
  "BusinessEntityID" INTEGER /* Employee identification number (Foreign key to employees). */,
  "LeaveTypeID" INTEGER /* Leave type identifier (Foreign key to leave_types). */,
  "StartDate" TEXT /* Date when the leave starts. */,
  "EndDate" TEXT /* Date when the leave ends. */,
  "NumberOfDays" INTEGER /* Number of days of leave taken */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("EmployeeLeaveID"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_leaves_BusinessEntityID FOREIGN KEY ("BusinessEntityID") REFERENCES employees ("BusinessEntityID"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_leaves_LeaveTypeID FOREIGN KEY ("LeaveTypeID") REFERENCES leave_types ("LeaveTypeID")
)
